summary: Production-grade, security-hardened ICP web marketplace for selling Rainbow Six Siege accounts, branded as 'Ascension's Market'. Replaces a Discord-based sales flow. Uses 4 canisters (Marketplace, Payment, Credential Vault, Vouch) with React+TypeScript frontend. Payments: ICP native + BTC/ETH via HTTPS outcalls to block explorers (Blockstream/Etherscan). Credential encryption: symmetric key in admin-controlled Credential Vault canister. Admin role: configurable principal updatable by current admin; first-login claimAdmin bootstrap supported.
architectural_notes: 4-canister separation: Marketplace, Payment, Credential Vault, Vouch — each with distinct responsibilities and access control
All sensitive logic lives in canisters; frontend is never trusted for access control decisions
Deny-by-default access control: validate caller principal on every sensitive canister method
Credentials stored encrypted at rest (symmetric key in Credential Vault); decrypted in memory only after buyer principal verification
BTC/ETH payments: 'send to address' flow with tx hash submission, verified via HTTPS outcalls to Blockstream (BTC) and Etherscan (ETH)
Admin role: configurable principal stored in canister, updatable only by current admin via authenticated call
Internet Identity used for buyer and admin authentication; principal ID is the identity anchor — no real names or emails stored
Bootstrap admin: first caller to invoke claimAdmin (when adminPrincipal is unset) is permanently granted admin; all subsequent admin changes require authorisation from the current admin
known_issues: BTC/ETH HTTPS outcalls to external block explorers introduce an external dependency; confirmations may be delayed or fail if the explorer API is down
Admin bootstrap issue: initial deployment did not grant admin to first login — claimAdmin bootstrap logic being added to fix this
Special Deals section/tab not appearing on marketplace page — under active investigation and fix
imageRequirements:
## Edit Images
- Replace the text R6 Market with Ascensions Market in the same bold military typeface keeping the dark near-black background with gold and red accents and the siege/shield emblem (marketplace-banner.dim_1200x300.png > ascensions-market-banner.dim_1200x300.png)

existing_features.title: Build the public-facing marketplace page with: (a) a hero/header section displaying the marketplace brand; (b) a rank filter bar allowing buyers to filter listings by rank (e.g., Copper, Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Champion); (c) a Special Deals section at the top showing listings with specialDeal = true, visually distinguished; (d) a listings grid showing cards for each account — each card displays rank, price, special deal badge if applicable, and availability status (Available/Sold); (e) sold accounts visually dimmed/disabled; (f) clicking an available listing opens a purchase flow modal; (g) all listing data fetched from the Marketplace canister public queries.

Build the purchase flow modal/page with: (a) step 1 — display selected account metadata (rank, price) and payment method selector (ICP, BTC, ETH); (b) step 2 — for ICP: display ICP payment instructions with the canister's receive address and the required memo, then a 'Verify Payment' button that calls the Payment canister's ICP verification function with the block index; for BTC: display a BTC address and expected amount, a transaction hash input field, and a 'Verify Payment' button that calls the BTC HTTPS outcall verification; for ETH: display an ETH address and expected amount, a transaction hash input field, and a 'Verify Payment' button that calls the ETH HTTPS outcall verification; (c) step 3 — on confirmed payment, display a 'View Credentials' button that calls getCredential and displays the decrypted credential in a secure, non-copy-paste-blocked view with a warning that the buyer should change credentials immediately; (d) after credentials are shown, automatically trigger the vouch prompt modal; (e) require Internet Identity login before initiating any purchase step.

Build the vouch prompt modal that: (a) appears automatically after credentials are delivered; (b) contains fields: anonymous username (text, required), rating (1–5 star selector, required), review text (textarea, optional); (c) rank is pre-filled from the purchased account; (d) on submit, calls the Vouch canister's submitVouch; (e) can be dismissed (skip) without submitting; (f) success state shows a confirmation message.

Build the public vouches/reviews page displaying: (a) all non-hidden vouches from getPublicVouches; (b) each vouch shows anonymous username, rank, star rating, review text, and timestamp; (c) no buyer principal or purchase ID is displayed; (d) overall average rating displayed at the top.

Build the Admin Dashboard (accessible only to authenticated users whose principal matches adminPrincipal) with the following sections: (a) Listings Management — table of all listings with columns for id, rank, price, specialDeal toggle, status, and actions (edit price, toggle special deal, delete); add new listing form with fields: rank selector, price, specialDeal toggle, encryptedCredentialRef (admin uploads pre-encrypted credential blob); (b) Upload Credentials — form to upload encrypted credential blob for a given accountId; (c) Purchases — read-only table of all purchase records (purchaseId, accountId, paymentMethod, txHash, status, timestamp); (d) Vouch Moderation — table of all vouches including hidden ones, with hide/unhide and delete actions; (e) Admin Settings — field to update the adminPrincipal; (f) If the authenticated user's principal does not match adminPrincipal, show an 'Access Denied' message and render nothing else.

Apply a dark, high-contrast visual theme across the entire frontend consistent with a premium gaming marketplace aesthetic: deep dark backgrounds (near-black), vivid accent colors inspired by Rainbow Six Siege's military/tactical visual language (e.g., amber/gold rank accents, crimson danger indicators), clean sans-serif typography, card-based listing layout with sharp borders, and subtle hover animations. Enforce this theme consistently across all pages and components.

Generate and use a custom marketplace logo/banner image: a dark tactical-themed banner featuring a stylised siege/shield emblem with the text 'R6 Market' in a bold military typeface, rendered on a near-black background with gold and red accents. Display this banner in the site header.

Expose a `claimAdmin` call from the frontend Admin Dashboard page: if the authenticated user is not currently admin and no admin has been set yet (backend returns a designated 'unclaimed' status), display a prominent 'Claim Admin' button. On click, call the `claimAdmin` backend function and refresh the admin status check. If an admin is already set and the current user is not that admin, continue showing the existing 'Access Denied' message.

Add a Rare Skins filter button to the marketplace rank filter bar, placed alongside the existing Unranked filter button. When Rare Skins is active, the listings grid filters to show only listings that have at least one tagged skin name. On each listing card, if rareSkinNames is non-empty, display the skin names as small badge chip tags beneath the rank; if empty, omit the skins section entirely from the card. If rareSkinShowcaseLink is set, display a small View Skins link on the card that opens the URL in a new tab.

In the Admin Dashboard Listings Management section, add a Rare Skins sub-panel for each listing. It must include: (1) a structured tag input field where the admin can type a skin name, press Enter to append it as a visible tag chip, and repeat; chips can be individually removed; (2) a text input for an optional external showcase link URL; (3) a Save button that calls the backend updateListing mutation with the updated rareSkinNames array and rareSkinShowcaseLink. The tag input must be available both when adding a new listing and when editing an existing one.

Investigate and fix why the Special Deals section is not appearing on the marketplace page. Audit the SpecialDealsSection component, the useGetSpecialDeals query hook, the backend getSpecialDeals query, and the rendering logic in MarketplacePage.tsx. Ensure the section renders whenever at least one listing has specialDeal = true, including the correct query key, stale time, and conditional rendering guard. Fix any bugs found in data fetching, response mapping, or conditional display.

Remove all Built with caffeine.ai branding text, links, and attribution from the entire frontend codebase. This includes the Footer component, any inline HTML, and any other component or page that references caffeine.ai or Built with. Do not replace it with any other attribution.

Rename the marketplace from R6 Market to Ascension's Market across the entire frontend. Update all occurrences in: page titles, Header component branding text, Footer brand name, hero section heading on MarketplacePage, the logo/banner image alt text and any rendered text overlay, Admin Dashboard page title, and any other string literal or constant referencing R6 Market.

Update the backend claimAdmin function so that: (1) if adminPrincipal is still the sentinel unclaimed value, the first authenticated non-anonymous caller is permanently granted adminPrincipal; (2) once an admin is set, any further claimAdmin call from a non-admin caller is rejected with an error; (3) an anonymous principal can never claim admin. Update the frontend Admin Dashboard so the Claim Admin button is shown only when no admin is yet set, and subsequent non-admin attempts show the Access Denied state.

new_feature_requests.summary: Marketplace Canister: public account listings (rank, price, special deal flag, availability only), rank filtering, special deals section, admin-controlled pricing and toggling
Payment Canister: ICP native ledger verification, BTC/ETH via HTTPS outcalls to Blockstream/Etherscan, purchase records, tx hash storage, credentials unlock only after on-chain confirmation
Credential Vault Canister: encrypted credentials at rest with admin-controlled symmetric key, strict buyer-only principal validation, no public query access, decrypt in memory on verified retrieval
Vouch Canister: purchase-verified review submission (one per purchase), anonymous username, rank, rating 1-5, text review, publicly visible, admin moderation (hide/delete)
React+TypeScript frontend: Internet Identity auth, rank-filterable listings, Special Deals section, secure post-payment credential delivery view, vouch prompt after credential delivery
Admin dashboard: add listings, upload encrypted credentials, set pricing, toggle special deals, view purchases, hide/delete vouches, update configurable admin principal

Implement the Marketplace canister actor in backend/main.mo with the following data model and logic: (a) AccountListing record containing id, rank, price (in e8s for ICP, or USD-equivalent for BTC/ETH), specialDeal flag, status (Available/Sold), and an encryptedCredentialRef opaque blob reference — NO username, email, or password fields exposed; (b) stable storage for all listings; (c) public query to list all listings (returns only public metadata — rank, price, specialDeal, status, id); (d) public query to filter listings by rank; (e) public query to get a single listing by id (public metadata only); (f) admin-only update calls to: add a listing, update listing price, toggle specialDeal flag, mark a listing Sold, delete a listing; (g) a dedicated query for the Special Deals section returning only listings where specialDeal is true; (h) all admin calls must validate caller principal against a stored adminPrincipal and trap/return error if not authorised; (i) updateAdmin call that allows the current admin to set a new adminPrincipal.

Implement the Payment canister logic (as a separate actor class or namespace within backend/main.mo, or as a second actor file if the build system supports it — otherwise as a clearly separated module within main.mo) with: (a) PurchaseRecord stable storage containing purchaseId, buyerPrincipal, accountId, paymentMethod (ICP/BTC/ETH), txHash, timestamp, status (Pending/Confirmed/Failed); (b) ICP payment verification: accept a block index / memo, call the ICP ledger canister (ryjl3-tyaaa-aaaaa-aaaba-cai) via inter-canister call to verify the transfer amount, recipient, and memo match the expected values for the listing price, then atomically mark the account Sold and create a Confirmed purchase record; (c) BTC payment verification: accept a txHash, call Blockstream API (https://blockstream.info/api/tx/{txid}) via HTTPS outcall, parse the response to confirm the output address and value match the expected BTC address and amount, store the txHash permanently, mark purchase Confirmed only after confirmation; (d) ETH payment verification: accept a txHash, call Etherscan API (https://api.etherscan.io/api?module=proxy&action=eth_getTransactionByHash&txhash={txhash}) via HTTPS outcall, parse response to confirm to-address and value match expected ETH address and amount, store txHash permanently, mark purchase Confirmed only after on-chain confirmation; (e) getPurchaseByBuyer query that returns purchase records ONLY if caller principal matches buyerPrincipal; (f) hasPurchased query returning Bool — true only if caller principal has a Confirmed purchase for the given accountId; (g) all sensitive queries validate caller principal explicitly.

Implement the Credential Vault logic within backend/main.mo with: (a) stable storage mapping accountId → encryptedCredentialBlob (encrypted by admin using a symmetric key before upload); (b) uploadCredential admin-only update call that stores an encrypted blob for a given accountId — validates caller is admin; (c) getCredential update call (must be update, not query, to enforce access control on-chain) that: validates caller is authenticated (non-anonymous principal), calls hasPurchased on the Payment module to confirm the caller has a Confirmed purchase for the requested accountId, and only then returns the encrypted blob; (d) the symmetric decryption key is stored separately and retrievable only by admin via an admin-authenticated getDecryptionKey call — the key is never returned to buyers; (e) credentials cannot be enumerated — no public list of credential refs; (f) rate-limit credential retrievals to a maximum of 5 views per purchaseId to prevent bulk exfiltration; (g) all access control checks performed inside the canister — frontend confirmation is never sufficient.

Implement the Vouch canister logic within backend/main.mo with: (a) Vouch record containing vouchId, purchaseId, buyerPrincipal, anonymousUsername, rank, rating (Nat 1–5), reviewText, timestamp, hidden (Bool, default false); (b) submitVouch update call that: validates caller is authenticated, validates caller has a Confirmed purchase for the given purchaseId, enforces one vouch per purchaseId (returns error if vouch already exists for that purchaseId), stores the vouch; (c) getPublicVouches query returning all vouches where hidden = false — returns anonymousUsername, rank, rating, reviewText, timestamp but NOT buyerPrincipal or purchaseId; (d) admin-only hideVouch and deleteVouch update calls that validate admin principal; (e) getMyVouches query returning vouches where caller principal matches buyerPrincipal.

Add a one-time admin bootstrap mechanism to the backend: if `adminPrincipal` has never been set (i.e., it holds a sentinel/anonymous value from initial deployment), the first authenticated caller of a new `claimAdmin` update function is automatically and permanently granted the `adminPrincipal` role. Subsequent calls to `claimAdmin` must be rejected once an admin is already set. All existing admin-only functions continue to require the caller to match the stored `adminPrincipal`.

Rare Skins section: placed alongside Unranked filter on marketplace page; admin can add external showcase link and tag individual skin names to a listing (structured tag input); skin names display on listing card, omitted if none tagged.
Fix Special Deals section/tab not appearing on the marketplace page.
Remove all 'Built with caffeine.ai' branding tags from the frontend.

Extend the backend AccountListing data model to include two new optional fields: (1) rareSkinNames — a list of text strings representing tagged skin names; (2) rareSkinShowcaseLink — an optional external URL string for a showcase link. Update all relevant admin update calls (addListing, updateListing) to accept and persist these new fields. Public listing queries must return these fields so the frontend can render them.
